key_themes: Consistent earnings beats with multiple upward revisions to FY2026 Adjusted EPS guidance (now $38.80–$39.20, implying 17–19% growth)
Strong momentum in oncology, specialty drug distribution, and biopharma services driving revenue and profit
Recent share price pullback from near $999 highs to ~$866 amid tactical analyst caution and GLP-1 pricing pressure concerns
Moderate Buy analyst consensus with average price targets of ~$970–$1,000 (12–15% upside)
Solid balance sheet moves (new credit facilities, dividend) alongside planned CFO transition
summary: McKesson (MCK) has delivered excellent operational results, repeatedly raising its fiscal 2026 guidance on the back of robust demand in specialty pharmaceuticals, oncology, and distribution services. The stock has pulled back modestly in recent weeks (currently ~$866, YTD +5.7%, 52-week range $637–$999), prompting a handful of target reductions and tactical underperform notes, yet the overall analyst consensus remains Moderate Buy/ Buy with meaningful upside to ~$970–$1,000 targets. Sentiment is solidly positive on fundamentals and execution ahead of the May 7 Q4 earnings release, with limited but generally supportive investor discussion on social media.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MCK/)[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/mckesson-corporation-nysemck-given-consensus-rating-of-moderate-buy-by-analysts-2026-04-16/)[[3]](https://www.mckesson.com/about-us/newsroom/press-releases/2026/mckesson-corporation-reports-fiscal-2026-third-quarter-results/)
sources: 20+ recent news articles, press releases, and analyst notes (Yahoo Finance, Seeking Alpha, Zacks, TipRanks, company IR), X posts (~15–20 scanned via keyword and semantic search; mostly low-engagement/promotional with 1–2 substantive comments on debt and earnings performance)
